---
title: "Acciones de recuperacion que terminan en tiro"
subtitle: "Ejemplo sencillo con StatsBomb Open Data"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

# Acciones de recuperación que terminan en tiro

Notebook sencillo para un partido de ejemplo.

Buscamos acciones de recuperación/intercepción/tackle que pertenecen a posesiones que acaban en tiro. Después enriquecemos esas acciones con minuto, igualdad numérica y estado de marcador del equipo que perdió el balón.

```{python}
import warnings

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from statsbombpy import sb

try:
    from IPython.display import display
except Exception:
    display = print

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 120)
pd.set_option("display.max_rows", 80)
```

## 1. Elegir partido

Usamos un partido de Bundesliga 2023/2024. Si quieres otro partido, cambia `MATCH_ID`.

```{python}
# Bundesliga 2023/2024
COMPETITION_ID = 9
SEASON_ID = 281

# None = usa el primer partido disponible de esa liga/temporada.
# Tambien puedes poner un match_id concreto.
MATCH_ID = None

matches = sb.matches(competition_id=COMPETITION_ID, season_id=SEASON_ID).copy()
matches = matches.sort_values(["match_date", "match_id"]).reset_index(drop=True)

if MATCH_ID is None:
    MATCH_ID = int(matches.loc[0, "match_id"])

match_info = matches[matches["match_id"] == MATCH_ID].copy()
home_team = match_info.iloc[0]["home_team"]
away_team = match_info.iloc[0]["away_team"]

print(f"MATCH_ID usado: {MATCH_ID}")
display(match_info[["match_id", "match_date", "home_team", "away_team", "home_score", "away_score"]])
```

## 2. Cargar eventos

Si falta alguna columna, la creamos con `pd.NA`. Esto evita que el notebook se rompa entre partidos con columnas distintas.

```{python}
COLS_NEEDED = [
    "id", "index", "period", "timestamp", "minute", "second",
    "type", "play_pattern", "possession", "possession_team", "team", "player",
    "location",
    "shot_outcome", "shot_statsbomb_xg",
    "ball_recovery_recovery_failure",
    "interception_outcome",
    "duel_type", "duel_outcome",
    # Para estimar igualdad numerica con rojas previas.
    "foul_committed_card", "bad_behaviour_card",
]


def has_xy(value):
    return isinstance(value, (list, tuple)) and len(value) >= 2


def get_x(value):
    return float(value[0]) if has_xy(value) else np.nan


def get_y(value):
    return float(value[1]) if has_xy(value) else np.nan


def prepare_events(raw_events):
    events = raw_events.copy()

    # Rellenamos columnas que no existan en este partido.
    for col in COLS_NEEDED:
        if col not in events.columns:
            events[col] = pd.NA

    events = events[COLS_NEEDED].copy()

    # Orden estable de eventos dentro del partido.
    fallback_order = pd.Series(np.arange(len(events)), index=events.index)
    events["event_order"] = pd.to_numeric(events["index"], errors="coerce").fillna(fallback_order)

    # Coordenadas StatsBomb: location = [x, y].
    events["x"] = events["location"].apply(get_x)
    events["y"] = events["location"].apply(get_y)

    events["period"] = pd.to_numeric(events["period"], errors="coerce").fillna(0).astype(int)
    events["minute"] = pd.to_numeric(events["minute"], errors="coerce")
    events["second"] = pd.to_numeric(events["second"], errors="coerce")
    events["possession"] = pd.to_numeric(events["possession"], errors="coerce")
    events["match_id"] = int(MATCH_ID)

    return events.sort_values(["period", "event_order"]).reset_index(drop=True)


raw_events = sb.events(match_id=MATCH_ID)
events = prepare_events(raw_events)

print(f"Eventos cargados: {len(events):,}")
events.head()
```

## 3. Filtrar acciones de recuperación

Este es el universo de acciones que nos interesa.

```{python}
TRANSITION_RECOVERY_SUCCESS = ["Won", "Success", "In Play", "Success In Play"]
TACKLE_SUCCESS = ["Won", "Success", "Success In Play"]


def build_recovery_actions(events):
    df_actions = events[events["type"].isin(["Ball Recovery", "Interception", "Duel"])].copy()

    mask_recovery = (
        (df_actions["type"] == "Ball Recovery")
        & (df_actions["ball_recovery_recovery_failure"].isna())
    )

    mask_interception = (
        (df_actions["type"] == "Interception")
        & (
            df_actions["interception_outcome"].astype("object").isin(TRANSITION_RECOVERY_SUCCESS)
            | df_actions["interception_outcome"].isna()
        )
    )

    mask_tackle = (
        (df_actions["type"] == "Duel")
        & (df_actions["duel_type"] == "Tackle")
        & (df_actions["duel_outcome"].astype("object").isin(TACKLE_SUCCESS))
    )

    recoveries = df_actions[mask_recovery | mask_interception | mask_tackle].copy()

    # Nos quedamos solo con acciones que tienen equipo, posesion y ubicacion.
    recoveries = recoveries.dropna(subset=["team", "possession", "x", "y"])
    recoveries["recovery_action_type"] = recoveries["type"]
    return recoveries


recovery_actions = build_recovery_actions(events)

print(f"Acciones de recuperación detectadas: {len(recovery_actions):,}")
recovery_actions[["minute", "second", "team", "player", "recovery_action_type", "possession", "x", "y"]].head(20)
```

## 4. Quedarnos con las que terminan en tiro

Una acción se queda si su misma posesión, para el mismo equipo, contiene un evento `Shot`.

```{python}
# Posesiones que contienen al menos un tiro.
shots = events[events["type"] == "Shot"].copy()

possessions_with_shot = (
    shots[["match_id", "possession", "team"]]
    .dropna(subset=["match_id", "possession", "team"])
    .drop_duplicates()
    .assign(ends_in_shot=1)
)

# Cruzamos recuperaciones con posesiones que acaban en tiro.
actions_leading_to_shot = recovery_actions.merge(
    possessions_with_shot,
    on=["match_id", "possession", "team"],
    how="inner",
).sort_values(["period", "event_order"]).reset_index(drop=True)

print(f"Tiros detectados: {len(shots):,}")
print(f"Acciones de recuperación cuya posesión termina en tiro: {len(actions_leading_to_shot):,}")
actions_leading_to_shot[["minute", "second", "team", "player", "recovery_action_type", "possession", "x", "y", "ends_in_shot"]]
```

## 5. Enriquecer las acciones

Añadimos tres cosas:

- `action_minute`: minuto decimal de partido.
- `is_numerical_equality`: igualdad numérica estimada antes de la acción.
- `losing_team_score_state`: si el equipo que perdió el balón iba ganando, empatando o perdiendo.

```{python}
def opponent_of(team):
    """En este partido, devuelve el rival de un equipo."""
    if team == home_team:
        return away_team
    if team == away_team:
        return home_team
    return pd.NA


# Tabla sencilla de goles para reconstruir el marcador antes de cada accion.
# Usamos Shot + Goal. Para empezar es suficiente y facil de revisar.
goals = (
    events[(events["type"] == "Shot") & (events["shot_outcome"] == "Goal")]
    [["period", "event_order", "team"]]
    .sort_values(["period", "event_order"])
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)


# Tabla sencilla de rojas para estimar jugadores disponibles antes de cada accion.
red_card_values = {"Red Card", "Second Yellow", "Second Yellow Card"}
red_cards = events[
    events[["foul_committed_card", "bad_behaviour_card"]]
    .apply(lambda row: any(str(value) in red_card_values for value in row.dropna()), axis=1)
][["period", "event_order", "team"]].copy()


def score_state_before(period, event_order, team):
    """Estado de marcador de un equipo antes de una accion."""
    rival = opponent_of(team)
    previous_goals = goals[
        (goals["period"] < period)
        | ((goals["period"] == period) & (goals["event_order"] < event_order))
    ]

    team_goals = int((previous_goals["team"] == team).sum())
    rival_goals = int((previous_goals["team"] == rival).sum())
    diff = team_goals - rival_goals

    if diff > 0:
        return "winning", diff
    if diff < 0:
        return "losing", diff
    return "drawing", diff


def numerical_equality_before(period, event_order, team):
    """Igualdad numerica antes de una accion, restando rojas previas."""
    rival = opponent_of(team)
    previous_reds = red_cards[
        (red_cards["period"] < period)
        | ((red_cards["period"] == period) & (red_cards["event_order"] < event_order))
    ]

    team_players = 11 - int((previous_reds["team"] == team).sum())
    rival_players = 11 - int((previous_reds["team"] == rival).sum())
    return team_players == rival_players, team_players, rival_players


rows = []
for _, action in actions_leading_to_shot.iterrows():
    recovery_team = action["team"]

    # Si este equipo recupera, asumimos que el rival es el equipo que acaba de perder el balon.
    losing_team = opponent_of(recovery_team)

    period = int(action["period"])
    event_order = float(action["event_order"])

    score_state, score_diff = score_state_before(period, event_order, losing_team)
    is_equal, losing_players, recovering_players = numerical_equality_before(period, event_order, losing_team)

    rows.append({
        "action_minute": round(float(action["minute"]) + float(action["second"]) / 60, 2),
        "recovery_team": recovery_team,
        "ball_losing_team": losing_team,
        "losing_team_score_state": score_state,
        "losing_team_score_diff": score_diff,
        "is_numerical_equality": is_equal,
        "ball_losing_team_players": losing_players,
        "recovering_team_players": recovering_players,
    })

context = pd.DataFrame(rows)
actions_enriched = pd.concat([actions_leading_to_shot.reset_index(drop=True), context], axis=1)

actions_enriched.head()
```

## 6. Dataframe final

Este es el dataframe limpio para explorar ubicaciones y contexto.

```{python}
result_cols = [
    "match_id", "period", "minute", "second", "action_minute", "event_order",
    "recovery_team", "ball_losing_team", "losing_team_score_state", "losing_team_score_diff",
    "is_numerical_equality", "ball_losing_team_players", "recovering_team_players",
    "player", "recovery_action_type", "possession", "x", "y", "play_pattern", "ends_in_shot",
]

recovery_locations_shot = actions_enriched[[c for c in result_cols if c in actions_enriched.columns]].copy()

print(f"Filas finales: {len(recovery_locations_shot):,}")
recovery_locations_shot
```

## 7. Comprobación rápida

Mostramos los tiros de las posesiones incluidas para comprobar que realmente acababan en tiro.

```{python}
shot_check = recovery_locations_shot[["match_id", "possession", "recovery_team"]].drop_duplicates().merge(
    shots[["match_id", "possession", "team", "minute", "second", "player", "shot_outcome", "shot_statsbomb_xg"]],
    left_on=["match_id", "possession", "recovery_team"],
    right_on=["match_id", "possession", "team"],
    how="left",
)

shot_check.drop(columns=["team"]).sort_values(["possession", "minute", "second"]).reset_index(drop=True)
```

## 8. Resumen simple

```{python}
summary = (
    recovery_locations_shot
    .groupby(["recovery_team", "ball_losing_team", "losing_team_score_state", "is_numerical_equality"], dropna=False)
    .agg(actions_leading_to_shot=("match_id", "count"))
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("actions_leading_to_shot", ascending=False)
)

summary
```
